Organizes hackathons on AI control, policy frameworks, and technical governance solutions. Collaborates with organizations like ControlAI on policy development and red-teaming exercises.

organizations.name: Athena Mentorship Program for Women (AI Alignment Research)
organizations.location: Remote program with rotating in-person retreats (previously Oxford UK, location varies)
organizations.notes: Founded 2023 by Claire Short. 10-week remote mentorship program with 1-week in-person retreat. Targets women transitioning into or early in AI alignment research careers. Funded through Manifund ($20k goal reached), Lightspeed grants. Requires 20+ hours/week commitment. Provides mentorship from established AI safety researchers, weekly research talks, networking opportunities, and potential placement in research groups post-program. Has run multiple cohorts with positive participant feedback.
organizations.threat_models: Loss of control, Existential risk
organizations.threat_models_detail: Program specifically targets technical AI alignment research, focusing on training women researchers to work on ensuring AI systems remain aligned with human values and controllable as they become more capable.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: Program focuses on technical AI alignment research capacity building rather than explicit policy positions. Aims to increase diversity in AI safety research field through mentorship and training.

organizations.name: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS) AI Policy Fellowship
organizations.notes: Nonpartisan think tank producing policy research on AI implications. Fellowship program runs 3 months with $15,000-$22,000 stipends, training professionals from varied backgrounds in AI policy. Alumni have secured positions at RAND, Institute for Progress, Center for Health Security, and government agencies. Recent research includes strategic visions in AI governance and policy recommendations for the AI Action Plan. Features mentorship from leading experts and produces concrete policy outputs like briefings to government officials and international conferences.
organizations.threat_models: National security, Power concentration, Loss of control, Weapons proliferation, Economic inequality
organizations.threat_models_detail: IAPS research specifically addresses three severe risks: takeover by powerful misaligned AI systems, wars resulting from competitive dynamics around AI, and AI-enabled concentration of power among a small group of people. Policy focus areas include CBRN capabilities of AI models, export controls, geopolitics of AI, and hardware-enabled governance mechanisms.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: IAPS focuses on practical policy solutions to address AI risks from today's advanced models to potential AGI and superintelligence, with emphasis on national security implications, government procurement, export controls, and international governance frameworks. Their research covers strategic visions for AI governance including government involvement and centralized development approaches.

organizations.name: Orion AI Governance Initiative (Arcadia Impact)
organizations.notes: Founded 2024 as part of Arcadia Impact. UK-based talent development scheme with 3 employees. Offers 9-week AI Policy Accelerator program, mentorship scheme, and internship placements. Has trained 57 participants across programs and placed 2 people into AI policy roles at think tanks. Applications for 2026 program closed January 6, 2026. Project manager is Callum Hinchcliffe, who previously worked on AI governance research and policy.
organizations.threat_models: Power concentration, National security, Loss of control, Economic inequality
organizations.threat_models_detail: Training program addresses governance challenges from general-purpose AI including capability regulation, power concentration from automation, and managing risks from the global AI arms race. Focus on frontier AI governance and policy questions.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: Program focuses on developing talent for AI governance careers with emphasis on managing risks from advanced AI systems. Curriculum covers policy levers including compute governance, model evaluations, and international AI governance frameworks.

organizations.name: Pathfinder Fellowship (Kairos)
organizations.notes: Launched 2025 by Kairos, replacing Open Philanthropy's University Organizer Fellowship and Kairos's prior Fieldbuilder Support Program. Supports ~70 AI safety university groups worldwide with funding, mentorship, and resources. 12 staff across 7 countries. Funded by Open Philanthropy grant, fiscally sponsored through BERI. Provides $500+ startup funding plus ongoing support and graduate student stipends.
organizations.threat_models: Loss of control, Existential risk, National security
organizations.threat_models_detail: Fellowship specifically supports groups working on technical AI safety, AI governance and policy, AI security, and AI welfare. Training focuses on preparing students for careers addressing large-scale risks from transformative AI systems.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: Program supports AI safety and policy university groups globally, training students to work on AI alignment, governance, and existential risk reduction. Mentors have experience at organizations like METR, RAND TASP, and US AISI, indicating focus on safety-first approaches to AI development.

organizations.name: Technical Alignment Research Accelerator (TARA)
organizations.location: Australia & New Zealand (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane) + Asia-Pacific expansion
organizations.notes: Founded 2024 by AI Safety Australia & New Zealand (AIS ANZ), led by Yanni Kyriacos. 14-week part-time program based on ARENA curriculum with weekly in-person Saturday sessions. Inaugural cohort targeted 25 participants across Australian cities. Funded by $14,901 grant from Ryan Kidd via Manifund. Expanding to Singapore, Manila, Tokyo, and Taipei in 2026. Program aims to develop technical talent for AI Safety Institutes and demonstrate community credibility to Australian government.
organizations.threat_models: Loss of control, Existential risk
organizations.threat_models_detail: Program focuses on technical AI safety and alignment research skills, specifically targeting prevention of the most harmful impacts of AI. Curriculum covers mechanistic interpretability, AI evaluations, and alignment techniques through the ARENA curriculum.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: Program explicitly aims to staff Australia's AI Safety Institute with safety-conscious technical talent and leverage Australia's strategic alliance with the US to promote robust global AI safety governance. Focus on preventing harmful impacts of AI through technical alignment research.

organizations.name: AI Futures Project
organizations.notes: Founded 2024, small research group of 5 people. Supported by charitable donations and grants. Key executives include Jonas Vollmer (COO) and Eli Lifland (Founding Researcher). Published the AI Futures Model in late 2025 - a quantitative model of AI timelines and takeoff scenarios. Team has backgrounds from Center for Human-Compatible AI, Global Priorities Institute (Oxford), and other AI safety organizations.
organizations.threat_models: Existential risk, Loss of control
organizations.threat_models_detail: Primary focus on forecasting superintelligent AI timelines and preparing society for rapid AI capability advancement. Research emphasizes that superintelligent AI could arrive much sooner than most expect, requiring urgent preparation efforts.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: Focuses on forecasting AI timelines and advocating for better preparation for superintelligent AI arrival. Believes current trends could lead to superintelligent AI much sooner than expected and that the world is not ready for this transition.

organizations.name: Center for AI Policy (CAIP)
organizations.notes: Founded 2023, shut down in September 2025 due to lack of funding. Was a 501(c)(4) advocacy organization that conducted over 406 meetings with Congressional staffers, hosted 8 congressional briefings reaching 150+ staffers, and developed the 'Responsible AI Act' model legislation. Funded primarily by Survival and Flourishing Fund. Executive Director Jason Green-Lowe continues to offer legislative review services and seeks funding to revive the organization.
organizations.threat_models: Loss of control, Existential risk, National security, Cybersecurity
organizations.threat_models_detail: Focused specifically on catastrophic and existential risks from advanced AI systems approaching or exceeding human intelligence. Their model legislation targeted risks from frontier AI systems that could pose unprecedented threats to public safety and national security through loss of human control.
organizations.regulatory_stance_detail: Advocated for comprehensive regulation of frontier AI systems through their 'Responsible AI Act' model legislation, requiring independent third-party audits, deployment permits, hardware security standards, and government oversight before powerful AI systems can be deployed. Focused on preventing catastrophic risks through mandatory safety evaluations.

resources.title: AI Frontiers
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Platform from the Center for AI Safety (CAIS) posting articles written by experts from a wide range of fields discussing the impacts of AI.

resources.title: AI Policy Bulletin
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Publishes policy-relevant perspectives on frontier AI governance, including research summaries, opinions, interviews, and explainers.

resources.title: AI Prospects

resources.title: Astral Codex Ten
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog covering many topics, including reasoning, science, psychiatry, medicine, ethics, genetics, economics, politics, and AI. Often book summaries and commentary on AI safety.

resources.title: Bounded Regret
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog on AI safety by Jacob Steinhardt, a UC Berkeley statistics professor, analysing risks, forecasting future breakthroughs, and discussing alignment strategies.

resources.title: ChinaTalk
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Deep, thoughtful coverage on China, technology, and US-China relations – written by Jordan Schneider.

resources.title: Cold Takes
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog about transformative AI, futurism, research, ethics, philanthropy etc. by Holden Karnofsky. Includes the "Most Important Century" post series. Last posted in 2024.

resources.title: Daniel Paleka's Newsletter
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by AI safety researcher Daniel Paleka curating and succinctly analyzing the latest research and news in AI safety. Posts about every two months.

resources.title: DeepMind Safety Research
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog from the Google DeepMind safety team discussing research ideas about building AI safely.

resources.title: Don't Worry about the Vase
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Zvi Mowshowitz on various topics, including AI, offering detailed analysis, personal insights, and a rationalist perspective. Posts very frequently.

resources.title: Miles's Substack
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog from ex-OpenAI (now independent) AI policy researcher Miles Brundage on the rapid evolution of AI and the urgent need for thoughtful governance.

resources.title: Obsolete
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Reporting and analysis on capitalism, great power competition, and the race to build superintelligence by freelance journalist Garrison Lovely.

resources.title: Paul Christiano's Blog
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog on aligning prosaic AI by one of the leading AI safety researchers. No longer active but the archive is high quality.

resources.title: Planned Obsolescence
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Substack by Ajeya Cotra, researcher at METR, covering AI capabilities forecasting, AI agent benchmarks, timelines for automating AI R&D, and implications for AI safety.

resources.title: Rising Tide
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Helen Toner (director of CSET and former OpenAI board member) offering analysis on navigating the transition to advanced AI systems.

resources.title: The Building Capacity Blog
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog about building the fields of AI safety and Effective Altruism, discussing big-picture strategy and sharing personal experience from working in the space.

resources.title: The Power Law
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Top forecaster Peter Wildeford forecasts the future and discusses AI, national security, innovation, emerging technology, and the powers – real and metaphorical – that shape the world.

resources.title: Threading the Needle
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Publication by Anton Leicht on the political economy of AI progress, featuring weekly posts on how institutions and political incentives interface with fast technological change.

resources.title: Victoria Krakovna's Blog
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Blog by an AI safety researcher at Google DeepMind, covering alignment research, rationality, and personal productivity insights. Last AI post was 2023.

resources.title: AI Safety in China
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter from Concordia AI, a Beijing-based social enterprise, providing updates on AI safety developments in China.

resources.title: AI Safety Newsletter
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter published every few weeks discussing recent developments in AI and AI safety. No technical background required.

resources.title: Can We Secure AI With Formal Methods?
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Current-events newsletter for keeping up to date with FMxAI (formal methods and AI) with a gear toward safety, doing a mix of shallow technical reviews of papers and movement updates.

resources.title: Import AI
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Weekly updates on the latest developments in AI research (including governance) written by Jack Clark, co-founder of Anthropic.

resources.title: ML Safety Newsletter
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Infrequent newsletter aiming to inform readers about recent ML safety research, focussing on areas like adversarial robustness, interpretability, and control.

resources.title: Transformer
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Aiming to help decision-makers understand what’s happening in AI and why it matters – through news roundups, explainers, features, and opinion pieces.

resources.title: 80,000 Hours Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. In-depth conversations about the world’s most pressing problems – including AI safety – and what you can do to help solve them.

resources.title: AI X-risk Research Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Interviews with (mostly technical) AI safety researchers about their research, aiming to get a sense of why it was written and how it might reduce existential risk from AI.

resources.title: Dwarkesh Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Well-researched interviews with influential intellectuals going in-depth on AI and technology, and their broader societal implications.

resources.title: Future of Life Institute Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Interviews with existential risk researchers, policy experts, philosophers, and a range of other influential thinkers.

resources.title: The AI Policy Podcast
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Podcast from the Center for Strategic & International Studies (CSIS) discussing AI regulation, innovation, national security, and geopolitics.

resources.title: The Cognitive Revolution
resources.notes: From AISafety.com map. Biweekly podcast where host Nathan Labenz interviews AI innovators and thinkers, discussing the transformative impact AI will likely have in the near future.

resources.title: AI Alignment Slack

resources.title: AI Digest

submissions.submitter_relationship: I can connect you with this person

llm_review.notes: Scott Wiener is a California State Senator focused on housing/transportation policy, not AI governance. The 'AGI in 2-3 years' claim is an extreme outlier unsupported by mainstream AI research and inconsistent with his actual policy record. Missing critical fields: affiliation, specific policy positions, source/date of data, and evidence supporting timeline claim. Appears to be either misclassified, fabricated, or from unreliable source.

Entry is well-formed with relevant metadata for a U.S. AI policy stakeholder. Scott Wiener is a California State Senator with legitimate AI policy involvement. Data fields are complete and reasonable. Minor consideration: 'Moderate' stance could benefit from citation or specificity, but is defensible given his mixed record on tech regulation.

submissions.resolution_notes: Accepted stance and timeline updates from colleague

llm_review.notes: Entry identifies a real AI policy stakeholder (CA State Senator Scott Wiener, known for tech regulation work), but lacks supporting documentation, verification source, and nuance. The 'Restrictive' stance is oversimplified—Wiener's actual positions are more nuanced (e.g., supports regulation but not blanket bans). Missing: affiliation details, specific policy positions, date submitted, and evidence for AGI timeline claim. Needs citation sources and more granular stance breakdown.
